title: nginx returns 403: permission denied serving files
description_md:
A teammate says the nginx web server on this box was working fine, but after a routine maintenance script ran last night, visitors now get an error page. SSH in as `trainee` (you have full passwordless `sudo`), figure out what's wrong, and get it serving traffic on port 80 again.

You'll know you're done when `curl localhost` returns nginx's default page with a 200 status.
